summary: Fix timeout enforcement via queryStream.interrupt() + 30s grace period, and fail-fast plugin validation after first session
context: Run 399777a7 revealed two infrastructure bugs: (1) timeout sessions ran 25-108 minutes because abort signal was soft and processStream() kept iterating, (2) Twining plugin silently failed to load — zero MCP tool calls across 72 twining-lite/full-twining sessions with no error logged. Both caused a 42-hour run that should have been ~12 hours, with invalid data for Twining conditions.
rationale: Timeout fix: after Promise.race rejects, call queryStream.interrupt() (SDK method) with a 30s grace period. This is the SDK's own cancellation mechanism — cleaner than process killing. Plugin fix: after first session of an iteration, if condition has plugins but zero Twining tool calls appeared, break the iteration loop with an error. Fail-fast prevents wasting 11 more sessions on invalid data.

alternatives.option: Hard-kill the SDK subprocess via process.kill()
alternatives.reason_rejected: SDK's interrupt() is the supported cancellation API. Process killing risks orphaned temp files and git locks.

alternatives.option: Validate plugin before session starts (check npx twining-mcp availability)
alternatives.reason_rejected: The SDK handles plugin loading internally — we can't pre-validate what the SDK will do. Post-first-session validation catches the actual failure mode.
